value.cross_market_context: normal regular session; European equities rallied to a record STOXX 600 close, led by healthcare with an AI-driven tech bounce that faded into the close, after the prior two-session pullback

text: European equity markets traded a normal regular session on Thursday, June 25, 2026, and closed broadly higher, with the pan-European STOXX 600 setting a record close. Live closing prints are evidenced by the LSEG FTSE 100 daily table (close as of 16:35 BST on volume of about 970 million shares), dated June 25 closes on the Trading Economics Germany and France pages, the Reuters European-close wrap, and the Il Sole 24 Ore close report. Daily moves are measured against the Wednesday, June 24 close. There is no cross-market calendar asymmetry: both European and US venues traded a normal session.

stoxx_europe_600.basis: Reuters cash close 640.21 / +0.8% (record close, intraday record 642.09); Trading Economics CFD EU600 638.86 / +0.58% (Jun/25); Reuters morning read about 638.11 / +0.46% by 0828 GMT; anchored to the Reuters cash close, TE-CFD divergence disclosed

euro_stoxx_50.basis: Trading Economics Jun/25 cross-index table 6,273.00 / +0.86% (previous 6,219.46); MarketScreener intraday widget about +0.68%; CFD-tracked

dax.basis: Trading Economics dated daily table DE40 24,944.30 / +0.82% (stats box Actual 24,942.30; previous 24,740.36); live CFD ticker about 24,949 / +0.84%; intraday news framed about +0.6% toward 24,900; CFD-tracked

cac_40.basis: Trading Economics stats box Actual and dated summary 8,448.70 / +0.75% (previous 8,385.49); later live CFD 8,426.70 / +0.49%; magnitude bracketed +0.49% to +0.75% on a cash-close versus CFD basis

market_session: European regular close on 2026-06-25; FTSE 100 at the 16:35 BST London close

calculation.method: STOXX 600: 640.21 minus 635.16 equals +5.05 (about +0.8%, Reuters cash close); EURO STOXX 50: 6,273.00 minus 6,219.46 equals +53.54 (+0.86%); DAX: 24,944.30 minus 24,740.36 equals +203.94 (+0.82%); CAC 40: 8,448.70 minus 8,385.49 equals +63.21 (+0.75%); FTSE 100: 10,529.89 minus 10,461.63 equals +68.26 (+0.65%); continental percentages are Trading Economics dated-table day-changes, the STOXX 600 is anchored to the Reuters cash close versus the Trading Economics CFD EU600

text: European equities closed broadly higher on Thursday, June 25, 2026. The pan-European STOXX Europe 600 rose about 0.8% to a record close of 640.21 (Reuters cash close, intraday record 642.09), while the Trading Economics CFD EU600 read 638.86 / +0.58% - a disclosed cash-close versus CFD divergence. The EURO STOXX 50 gained 0.86% to 6,273.00, Germany's DAX added 0.82% to 24,944.30 (from 24,740.36), and France's CAC 40 rose 0.75% to 8,448.70 on the stats-box close with a later live CFD at 8,426.70 / +0.49%. The UK's FTSE 100 added 0.65% to a precise 10,529.89. Daily moves are measured against the June 24 close.

market_session: London regular session close, Thursday 2026-06-25 (16:35 BST)

calculation.method: close 10,529.89 minus prior close 10,461.63 equals +68.26 points; +68.26 / 10,461.63 equals +0.65 percent

text: The FTSE 100 is the cycle's one precise official close and a solid gainer: on the Investors Chronicle/LSEG historical daily table it opened 10,461.52, traded a 10,415.45 to 10,575.31 range, and closed 10,529.89 on volume of 970,238,545 shares, up 68.26 points (+0.65%) from Wednesday's 10,461.63. London advanced with the broader European rally and set a fresh intraday high of 10,575.31.

subject.name: AI rally reignited by Micron and Qualcomm, a Bayer-led healthcare surge, and an oil reversal

value.primary_support: strong Micron and Qualcomm forecasts reignited the global AI rally and lifted European chip and tech names, while a Bayer surge drove healthcare and easing-then-reversing oil framed the macro backdrop
triggers: Micron and Qualcomm earnings and forecasts after the Wednesday US close
US Supreme Court reining in Bayer Roundup lawsuits
oil reversing from intraday lows to gains on a Strait of Hormuz fees report
US inflation above 4% in May keeping Fed and ECB hikes in play
value.tech: European tech (up 32% for the quarter) jumped as much as 2.5% intraday before fading to +0.8% at the close
value.rates: traders priced Fed and ECB rate hikes by year-end per LSEG data; 10-year Treasury yield up about 1 bp, German 10-year flat
value.oil: Brent fell about 1.2% to 72.8 dollars intraday then reversed to about +1.5% (74.8 dollars) by the European close

text: European shares rallied to a record STOXX 600 close on Thursday, led by healthcare and a revived AI trade. Strong forecasts from Micron and Qualcomm after the US close - Micron citing 22 billion dollars of committed memory-chip orders and Qualcomm 15 billion dollars of data-centre sales by 2029 - reignited the global AI rally, lifting Asian and European chipmakers; Europe's tech sector rose as much as 2.5% before fading to +0.8% at the close. Healthcare was the standout as Bayer jumped 18.7% to top the STOXX 600 after the US Supreme Court reined in Roundup cancer lawsuits. Oil swung from an intraday decline (Brent about -1.2% as tankers exited the Strait of Hormuz) to a gain after a report Tehran planned to charge fees to reopen the strait. US inflation broke above 4% in May, keeping Fed and ECB hikes priced by year-end.

leaders.healthcare_note: biggest boost to the STOXX 600; Bayer +18.7% on the US Supreme Court Roundup ruling
leaders.technology_note: closed +0.8% after gaining as much as 2.5% intraday; STOXX 600 tech sector index +0.9% per Il Sole; chipmakers led

laggards.aerospace_and_defence_note: defence at the bottom of the tape - Avio -6.62%, Leonardo -3.97%, Fincantieri -2.92% in Milan, Rheinmetall -1.78% in Frankfurt, Thales -2.71% at the Paris open
laggards.banks_note: Euro Stoxx Banks roughly flat at +0.09%; BNP Paribas +0.83%
laggards.automobiles_note: Stellantis -0.95% on weak European vehicle registrations, while Volkswagen rose on the Everllence sale

text: The tape rotated into healthcare and technology and out of defence. Per Reuters, healthcare was among the biggest boosts to the STOXX 600, up 1.5%, on Bayer's 18.7% jump, while the tech sector closed up 0.8% after gaining as much as 2.5% intraday (Il Sole put the STOXX 600 tech sector index at +0.9%). Defence was the clear laggard, with Milan's Avio -6.62%, Leonardo -3.97% and Fincantieri -2.92%, Frankfurt's Rheinmetall -1.78% and Paris's Thales -2.71% at the open. Banks were roughly flat (Euro Stoxx Banks +0.09%) and Stellantis fell 0.95% on weak European registrations. Sector percentages are carried at medium confidence; index-level closes are high confidence.

gainers.Bayer_note: topped the STOXX 600 on the US Supreme Court Roundup ruling
gainers.Infineon_basis: Reuters cash close +3.1%; Trading Economics component +5.46%; Reuters morning +5.6%
gainers.Volkswagen_note: agreed to sell Everllence diesel-engine unit to Bain Capital for about 7.4 billion euros (8.4 billion dollars)

decliners.SAP_basis: Trading Economics component -1.04% versus the page biggest-laggards note -2.1%

text: The day's standout was Bayer, up 18.7% to top the STOXX 600 after the US Supreme Court reined in Roundup cancer lawsuits. Chipmakers led the AI bounce - at the Reuters cash close Infineon +3.1%, STMicroelectronics +5.1%, ASML +2.6% and Siemens Energy +2.3%, with the Trading Economics CFD components higher (Infineon +5.46%) on a disclosed basis. Other gainers: 3i Group +11.4% on Action's sales, EasyJet +6.4% after rejecting a fourth Castlelake bid, Volkswagen about +1.3% to +1.5% on selling Everllence to Bain Capital for about 7.4 billion euros, plus Milan's STMicroelectronics +2.75%, Ferrari +1.58% and Enel +2%. Decliners were led by defence - Avio -6.62%, Leonardo -3.97%, Fincantieri -2.92%, Rheinmetall -1.78% - alongside Fresenius Medical Care -4.3%, Deutsche Boerse -2.1%, SAP (-1.04% to -2.1% by basis) and Stellantis -0.95%. Single-stock percentages are carried at medium confidence with cash-close versus CFD basis disclosed.

oil.note: declined as tankers exited the Strait of Hormuz, then reversed on a report Tehran would charge fees to reopen it

rates.us_10y: +1 bp after a 9 bp drop the prior day
rates.german_10y: flat, down 11 bps on the week
rates.pricing: Fed and ECB hikes priced by year-end per LSEG data

inflation.us_may: above 4% for the first time in three years

fx.euro: about 1.1367 to 1.138 dollars, near a 13-month low

gold.note: slid below 4,000 dollars for the first time in 2026 (about 3,995) then recovered to about 4,028

value.france_consumer_confidence: rose to 84 in June from 82, above the 83 expected

text: The cross-asset backdrop was constructive but two-sided. Oil fell intraday (Brent about -1.2% to 72.8 dollars) as stranded tankers exited the Strait of Hormuz, then reversed to a gain (Brent about +1.5% to 74.8 dollars) after a report Tehran planned to charge fees to reopen the strait. Easing rate pressure rallied bonds: the US 10-year yield was up about 1 bp after a 9 bp drop, and the German 10-year was flat (down 11 bps on the week). US inflation broke above 4% in May for the first time in three years while PCE came in line, keeping Fed and ECB hikes priced by year-end. The euro sat near a 13-month low around 1.137 to 1.138 dollars with the dollar near a one-year high, gold slid below 4,000 dollars for the first time in 2026 before recovering toward 4,028, and France's consumer confidence rose to 84 in June, above expectations.
